reasoning: The Artemis Accords have maintained a consistent expansion rate of approximately one new signatory per month since their inception (55 signatories in 56 months). Historical data shows that June is a peak month for signings, likely due to major international diplomatic and aerospace events; specifically, June saw signings in 2021 (Brazil), 2023 (Ecuador and India), and 2024 (Armenia). In June 2025, two major venues for such diplomatic announcements are scheduled: the Paris Air Show (June 16-22) and the 68th session of the UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (starting June 25). Crucially, a NASA statement on May 22, 2025, explicitly noted that the agency 'anticipates additional countries will sign in the coming weeks and months,' aligning perfectly with the June 2025 window. With current momentum (Bangladesh signing in April; Norway in May) and the alignment of these events, the probability of a signing in June is very high.
